title: JavaScript Essentials
description: Learn JavaScript from variables and functions through closures, promises, async/await, and modern ES6+ features.

modules.title: Basics & Types
modules.description: Variables, types, operators, and control flow.

sections.title: let vs const vs var
sections.explanation: `let` allows reassignment, `const` does not, `var` is function-scoped. a is reassigned to 10.

sections.title: Strict Equality
sections.explanation: `==` performs type coercion (0 equals '0'). `===` checks type AND value (number !== string).

sections.title: Typeof
sections.explanation: This is a well-known JavaScript quirk. `typeof null` returns 'object' due to a legacy bug in the language.

sections.title: Array Methods
sections.explanation: `filter` keeps even numbers [2,4], then `map` multiplies by 10 → [20, 40].

modules.title: Functions & Scope
modules.description: Closures, arrow functions, hoisting, and scope.

sections.title: Closures
sections.explanation: The inner arrow function 'closes over' the `count` variable. Each call increments and returns the updated value.

sections.title: Arrow vs Regular this
sections.body_markdown: In JavaScript, how does `this` differ in arrow functions vs regular functions?

choices.A: Arrow functions have their own `this` binding
choices.B: Arrow functions inherit `this` from the enclosing lexical scope
choices.C: Regular functions inherit `this` from the parent scope

sections.explanation: Arrow functions do not have their own `this` — they capture `this` from the surrounding scope at definition time.

sections.title: Hoisting
sections.explanation: `var` declarations are hoisted (moved to top of scope), but the assignment happens at the original line. So x is undefined when logged.

sections.title: Destructuring
sections.code_snippet:
const { a, b, ...rest } = { a: 1, b: 2, c: 3, d: 4 };
console.log(rest);
sections.explanation: Destructuring extracts `a` and `b`; the rest operator `...rest` collects remaining properties into a new object.

sections.title: Spread Operator
sections.explanation: The spread operator `...` expands arrays into individual elements, creating a flat merged array.

modules.title: Async JavaScript
modules.description: Promises, async/await, and the event loop.

sections.title: Promise Basics
sections.code_snippet:
const p = new Promise((resolve) => resolve(42));
p.then(val => console.log(val));
sections.explanation: The promise resolves immediately with 42. `.then()` receives the resolved value and logs it.

sections.title: async/await
sections.explanation: `async` functions always return a Promise. Returning 'data' is equivalent to `Promise.resolve('data')`.

sections.title: Event Loop Order
sections.explanation: Sync code runs first (A, D). Microtasks (Promises) run before macrotasks (setTimeout). So C before B.

sections.title: Error Handling in Promises
sections.explanation: `.catch()` handles the rejection and returns 'oops!', which becomes the resolved value for `.then()`.

modules.title: ES6+ & Node
modules.description: Modern syntax, modules, and npm basics.

sections.title: Optional Chaining
sections.explanation: Optional chaining `?.` short-circuits to `undefined` if any part of the chain is null/undefined, avoiding errors.

sections.title: Nullish Coalescing
sections.explanation: `??` only falls through on null/undefined (0 is kept). `||` falls through on any falsy value (0 is falsy).

sections.title: npm Basics

choices.B: Installs express and adds it to package.json dependencies
choices.C: Creates a new project called express

sections.explanation: `npm install <pkg>` downloads the package to node_modules. `--save` (default in npm 5+) adds it to package.json.

sections.title: Import / Export
sections.explanation: Default imports get the `export default` value. Named imports in `{}` get named exports.

sections.title: Reverse String Challenge
sections.body_markdown: Read a line from stdin and print it reversed.
